rss.title: xAI grows data center gas fleet despite legal scrutiny – This Week in Cleantech
rss.snippet: On this episode of the This Week in Cleantech podcast, Molly Taft from WIRED discusses Elon Musk's AI company xAI's decision to add 19 natural gas turbines to its Colossus 2 data center in Southaven, Mississippi.

rss.title: The EPA is expediting clean air permits for power plants to remove ‘unnecessary delays’
rss.snippet: The U.S. Environmental Protection Agency is expediting Clean Air Act permit reviews to eliminate delays, allowing concurrent public comment and review.

rss.title: Elective pay explained as key IRS deadline approaches | Q&A with tax credit specialist Bryen Alperin
rss.snippet: Municipalities, schools, and first-time applicants are accelerating projects under the IRS's elective pay provision before the May 15 deadline.

arxiv_papers.title: Price Distortions in Korea's Electricity Market: Barriers to Renewable Integration and Reform Pathways
arxiv_papers.snippet: Structural distortions in price signals within the Korean electricity market, governed by a cost-based pool (CBP) and a uniform pricing mechanism, fundamentally undermine the nation's energy transition goals. arxiv_papers.snippet: In power systems, the risk of wildfire ignition has increased significantly in recent years. youtube_items.title: [YT] The Green Energy Revolution: How Solar & Wind Are Changing Everything (2025)
youtube_items.summary: The numbers tell a story that seemed impossible just a decade ago. In 2024, for the first time in history, the world invested more in solar energy than in all fossil fuels combined. Solar power installations are now growing at a pace that consistently shatters expert predictions. What we are witnessing is not merely an energy transition. It is the fastest global technological and industrial transformation in human history. This is the story of how solar and wind energy went from expensive, subsidized alternatives to the cheapest sources of electricity humanity has ever known. And how this shift is fundamentally rewriting the rules of global power. The revolution began with a simple, relentless curve. youtube_items.title: [YT] The Energy Revolution is Unstoppable: Record Solar and Wind Additions in 2025
youtube_items.summary: The world added a staggering amount of wind and solar power in 2025 and the numbers were incredible. Hello my friends, welcome to the channel, I'm Sam Evans and this is video number 8100. I've done many videos about wind and solar and batteries and how important they are to the planet, but what just happened in 2025 is truly, truly astonishing. New data from global energy think tank embers shows that 814 gigawatts of new solar and wind capacity came one line last year, which was an increase of 17% from 2024's 696 gigawatts. That brings total global wind and solar capacity to 4,170 gigawatts or just over 4 terawatts. That sounds like a lot and it is, it's huge.
